title: U.S. Large Models Copied DeepSeek's Work: Performance Not Better, Prices Higher, but Precisely Met the Compliance Needs of U.S. Enterprises
excerpt: OpenAI's former Chief Technology Officer Murati's first work after leaving her former employer has unexpectedly reversed the roles of China and the US in large
content_html:
OpenAI's former Chief Technology Officer Murati's first work after leaving her former employer has unexpectedly reversed the roles of China and the US in large model development. On July 15, she founded Thinking Machines Lab and released its first model, Inkling. The hybrid expert architecture mainly follows DeepSeek-V3, and for post-training cold start, it also used synthetic data generated by open models such as Moonshot's Kimi K2.5. The results are quite subtle: In multiple benchmark evaluations, Inkling lags behind Kimi and GLM, and its calling price is even higher. A star company that raised $2 billion and was valued at $12 billion, its debut was somewhat below expectations, but Murati may have never intended to compete for the top spot from the beginning.

Looking at the hardware configuration alone, Inkling indeed lives up to the image of a star lab. It uses a hybrid expert architecture with a total parameter count of 975 billion, activating 41 billion parameters per token processed; pre-training used 45 trillion tokens, covering text, images, audio, and video, supporting up to 1 million tokens of context; it can understand text, images, and audio, outputting in text form, and supports adjusting thinking intensity to balance performance, speed, and cost. The model also opens its weights, using a relatively liberal Apache 2.0 license, allowing developers to download weights for self-deployment or fine-tune through Thinking Machines' Tinker platform. The company's positioning is clear: rather than directly competing with ChatGPT for ordinary users, Inkling is more suitable as a foundation for enterprises developing AI applications.

What really caught attention is hidden in the latter part of the technical report. Thinking Machines explicitly stated in the architecture introduction that Inkling's hybrid expert design mainly followed DeepSeek-V3, with a large number of expert modules, where each token only activates a small portion, and it also adopted DeepSeek-V3's no-assistant loss load balancing design. The influence of Chinese models extended into the training phase: to initiate post-training, the company first performed a round of supervised fine-tuning using synthetic data generated by open-weight models, explicitly mentioning Kimi K2.5.

A complete roadmap emerges— an American laboratory founded by OpenAI's former CTO, its first model follows DeepSeek architecture, and then starts post-training with Kimi's data. Borrowing from open models itself is not problematic, as DeepSeek and Kimi publicly release weights and allow usage, which is built upon public achievements. More awkwardly, after absorbing the technology of Chinese models, Inkling still did not surpass these teachers. Thinking Machines also candidly admits: Inkling is not currently the strongest model, whether open or closed-source.

When the numbers are laid out clearly, it becomes even clearer. In the final human exam pure text evaluation, Inkling scored 29.7%, while Kimi K2.6 and GLM 5.2 reached 35.9% and 40.1%, respectively; after adding tools, Inkling rose to 46%, still lower than Kimi K2.6's 54% and GLM 5.2's 54.7%; in SWE-Bench Pro, which tests real software engineering capabilities, Inkling scored 54.3%, while Kimi K2.6 was 58.6%, and GLM 5.2 reached 62.1%; the gap was most glaring in Terminal Bench 2.1, where Inkling scored 63.8%, while Kimi K2.6 and GLM 5.2 were 71.3% and 82.7%, respectively.

Of course, Inkling has strengths in web application design, audio understanding, and security evaluation, and it can even exceed Kimi and DeepSeek in some math tasks. However, in terms of comprehensive reasoning, programming, and agent capabilities, it hasn't yet made it into the top tier of open models. Its pricing also didn't bring surprises: on the Tinker platform, the 64K version charges $1.87 per 1 million Prefill Tokens and $4.68 per 1 million Sample Tokens, and this is already a five-day discounted price. The 256K version rises to $3.74 and $9.36, respectively; compared to Kimi K2.6's official API input of $0.95 and output of $4, and GLM 5.2's $1.4 and $4.4, Inkling's prefill price is nearly twice that of Kimi K2.6, and its generation price is also higher, showing no obvious price advantage. Thus, the situation becomes a bit delicate: the architecture references DeepSeek, the post-training relies on Kimi, the overall performance doesn't exceed China's leading open models, and the calling price is even higher.

The reason this is intriguing lies in the unique background of Thinking Machines. The company is almost an OpenAI alumni association— when it was introduced in February 2025, the team had about 30 people, two-thirds came from OpenAI, and the rest mainly from Meta and Mistral. The founding team once included John Schulman, Barret Zoph, Lilian Weng, Andrew Tulloch, and Luke Metz, and Reuters reported that Murati had recruited at least 20 researchers from her former employer.

Murati joined OpenAI in 2018, participated in DALL-E, Codex, ChatGPT, and Sora, was promoted to CTO in 2022, and briefly served as interim CEO after the board dismissed Altman in November 2023, gaining full insight into OpenAI's model development and productization path. Such a group of people who know OpenAI best left to build a technical system from scratch, and the outside world expected a route marked by OpenAI's imprint. However, the most obvious technical source turned out to point to Chinese models, which could easily be interpreted as a dramatic signal: the people who knew OpenAI best ultimately chose the technical blueprint of Chinese models.

But this conclusion needs to be analyzed carefully. OpenAI's recent cutting-edge models are all closed-source, so the outside world cannot obtain weights or understand the complete architecture and training plan. Even if Murati was familiar with internal technologies, she couldn't transfer OpenAI's commercial secrets into the new company; however, DeepSeek and Kimi have publicly released weights or technical reports, and their architectures, training methods, and toolchains can be legally researched and reused.

Inkling itself is also an open-weight model, and for companies wanting to build an open ecosystem, adopting mature solutions from open models like DeepSeek and Kimi is a logical engineering choice. Today, developing an open model while completely avoiding the publicly available achievements of Chinese teams would require a higher cost of trial and error. Therefore, drawing inspiration from Chinese models cannot prove that Murati believes DeepSeek is superior to OpenAI. The differences in openness and the conditions for reference are completely different between the two, but it sends a clear signal: at least in the field of open weights, Chinese models have become an important reference for American AI teams.

The real question is why Thinking Machines, having absorbed existing architectures and training experiences, produced an Inkling with weaker performance and higher prices. Murati has a top team, sufficient funds, and the latest NVIDIA training system. There is no reason for her to overlook these gaps, so the high-cost alternative might be a calculated choice.

Considering the treatment Thinking Machines received, Inkling shouldn't just be a decent model. In July 2025, the company completed a $2 billion seed round before launching any product, with a valuation of $12 billion, and investors include a16z, NVIDIA, AMD, and Sequoia. Four months later, the next round of negotiations aimed for a valuation of up to $500 billion, although not officially announced, the company was already seen as a potential competitor to OpenAI and Anthropic. When it launched a product that performed worse than Chinese models and was even more expensive, it inevitably fell short of expectations. But from a business perspective, Murati may have never intended to win the benchmarks from the beginning.

The company emphasizes open weights, multimodality, and customizability, hoping that enterprises will use Tinker to fine-tune Inkling and turn it into specialized models such as customer service, coding assistants, and industry agents. Murati's bet is on a market that does not prioritize benchmark rankings. Enterprises care more about whether the model can be privately deployed, whether they can control the data, and whether they can continue training according to their own business. A large portion of this demand has been met by Chinese open models: OpenAI and Anthropic have long been closed-source, and after Meta's Llama4 did not meet expectations, it gradually reduced its open-source approach. Meanwhile, DeepSeek, Kimi, Qwen, and GLM continuously released weights, with performance increasingly close to American closed-source models and much lower prices.

US enterprises have quickly voted with their feet— according to data provided by OpenRouter to CNBC, since February 2026, the weekly share of Chinese models in tokens called by US enterprises through the platform has exceeded 30% every week, reaching as high as 46%, while the average share in the first half of 2025 was only 4.5%. Cursor tested multiple base models when developing Composer2 and finally chose Kimi K2.5, which had the strongest evaluation. Bridgewater Fund also fine-tuned Alibaba's Qwen through Tinker, achieving better results than some top closed-source models. Thus, Chinese open models have become a cost-saving shortcut for US enterprises, but this path is now under pressure.

At the same time, the regulatory atmosphere in the US toward Chinese AI models is tightening. Relevant departments have issued warnings about data security, model sources, and supply chain risks, and some companies using Chinese models have faced investigations. Even without unified restrictions, policy uncertainty has been enough to affect enterprise choices, especially for companies involving government business and sensitive data.

This is exactly the market space for Inkling: it is an open-weight model developed by an American company, using the Apache 2.0 license, supporting private deployment and customization. US enterprises choose it, without worrying about political controversies caused by using Chinese models, and it is easier to pass compliance reviews from government clients and large companies. The real value of Inkling comes from this compliance certainty— Murati transformed the public achievements of DeepSeek and Kimi into a model provided by an American company. It doesn't need to defeat Chinese models in benchmarks, but only needs to be an acceptable choice for US enterprises that dare not continue using Chinese models. This also explains why it dares to set a higher price: the policy has narrowed the competitive scope, and the gap in performance and price is less fatal. For some US enterprises, a slightly weaker and more expensive model is already sufficient.

sentences.zh: OpenAI前首席技术官Murati离开前雇主后的第一份工作，意外地颠倒了中国和美国在大型模型开发中的角色。

sentences.en: On July 15, she founded Thinking Machines Lab and released its first model, Inkling.
sentences.zh: 7月15日，她创立了Thinking Machines Lab，并发布了第一个模型Inkling。

sentences.en: The hybrid expert architecture mainly follows DeepSeek-V3, and for post-training cold start, it also used synthetic data generated by open models such as Moonshot's Kimi K2.5.
sentences.zh: 混合专家架构主要遵循DeepSeek-V3，对于训练后冷启动，它还使用了Moonshot的Kimi K2.5等开放模型生成的合成数据。

sentences.en: The results are quite subtle: In multiple benchmark evaluations, Inkling lags behind Kimi and GLM, and its calling price is even higher.
sentences.zh: 结果相当微妙：在多项基准评估中，Inkling 落后于 Kimi 和 GLM，而且其调用价格甚至更高。

sentences.en: A star company that raised $2 billion and was valued at $12 billion, its debut was somewhat below expectations, but Murati may have never intended to compete for the top spot from the beginning.
sentences.zh: 一家融资20亿美元、估值120亿美元的明星公司，上市首秀有些低于预期，但穆拉蒂可能从一开始就没打算争夺头把交椅。

sentences.zh: 该公司的定位很明确：与其直接与普通用户的ChatGPT竞争，Inkling更适合作为企业开发AI应用的基础。

blocks.html_en: Of course, Inkling has strengths in web application design, audio understanding, and security evaluation, and it can even exceed Kimi and DeepSeek in some math tasks. However, in terms of comprehensive reasoning, programming, and agent capabilities, it hasn't yet made it into the top tier of open models. Its pricing also didn't bring surprises: on the Tinker platform, the 64K version charges $1.87 per 1 million Prefill Tokens and $4.68 per 1 million Sample Tokens, and this is already a five-day discounted price. The 256K version rises to $3.74 and $9.36, respectively; compared to Kimi K2.6's official API input of $0.95 and output of $4, and GLM 5.2's $1.4 and $4.4, Inkling's prefill price is nearly twice that of Kimi K2.6, and its generation price is also higher, showing no obvious price advantage. Thus, the situation becomes a bit delicate: the architecture references DeepSeek, the post-training relies on Kimi, the overall performance doesn't exceed China's leading open models, and the calling price is even higher.

sentences.en: Of course, Inkling has strengths in web application design, audio understanding, and security evaluation, and it can even exceed Kimi and DeepSeek in some math tasks.
sentences.zh: 当然，Inkling 在 Web 应用程序设计、音频理解和安全评估方面具有优势，在某些数学任务中甚至可以超过 Kimi 和 DeepSeek。

sentences.en: However, in terms of comprehensive reasoning, programming, and agent capabilities, it hasn't yet made it into the top tier of open models.
sentences.zh: 但在综合推理、编程、代理能力方面，还没有进入开放模型的顶尖水平。

sentences.en: Its pricing also didn't bring surprises: on the Tinker platform, the 64K version charges $1.87 per 1 million Prefill Tokens and $4.68 per 1 million Sample Tokens, and this is already a five-day discounted price.
sentences.zh: 其定价也没有带来惊喜：在 Tinker 平台上，64K 版本的收费为每 100 万个 Prefill Token 1.87 美元，每 100 万个 Sample Token 收费 4.68 美元，而且这已经是五天折扣价了。

sentences.en: The 256K version rises to $3.74 and $9.36, respectively; compared to Kimi K2.6's official API input of $0.95 and output of $4, and GLM 5.2's $1.4 and $4.4, Inkling's prefill price is nearly twice that of Kimi K2.6, and its generation price is also higher, showing no obvious price advantage.
sentences.zh: 256K 版本分别上涨至 3.74 美元和 9.36 美元；相比Kimi K2.6官方API输入0.95美元、输出4美元，以及GLM 5.2的1.4美元和4.4美元，Inkling的预充价格是Kimi K2.6的近两倍，其代价也更高，没有表现出明显的价格优势。

sentences.en: Thus, the situation becomes a bit delicate: the architecture references DeepSeek, the post-training relies on Kimi, the overall performance doesn't exceed China's leading open models, and the calling price is even higher.
sentences.zh: 这样一来，情况就变得有些微妙了：架构参考了DeepSeek，后期训练依赖Kimi，整体性能没有超过国内领先的开放模型，而且调用价格更高。

blocks.html_en: The reason this is intriguing lies in the unique background of Thinking Machines. The company is almost an OpenAI alumni association— when it was introduced in February 2025, the team had about 30 people, two-thirds came from OpenAI, and the rest mainly from Meta and Mistral. The founding team once included John Schulman, Barret Zoph, Lilian Weng, Andrew Tulloch, and Luke Metz, and Reuters reported that Murati had recruited at least 20 researchers from her former employer.

sentences.en: The reason this is intriguing lies in the unique background of Thinking Machines.
sentences.zh: 之所以有趣，是因为思维机器的独特背景。

sentences.en: The company is almost an OpenAI alumni association— when it was introduced in February 2025, the team had about 30 people, two-thirds came from OpenAI, and the rest mainly from Meta and Mistral.
sentences.zh: 该公司几乎是一个 OpenAI 校友会——2025 年 2 月成立时，团队约有 30 人，三分之二来自 OpenAI，其余主要来自 Meta 和 Mistral。

sentences.en: The founding team once included John Schulman, Barret Zoph, Lilian Weng, Andrew Tulloch, and Luke Metz, and Reuters reported that Murati had recruited at least 20 researchers from her former employer.
sentences.zh: 创始团队曾包括 John Schulman、Barret Zoph、Lilian Weng、Andrew Tulloch 和 Luke Metz，据路透社报道，Murati 从她的前雇主那里招募了至少 20 名研究人员。

blocks.html_en: Murati joined OpenAI in 2018, participated in DALL-E, Codex, ChatGPT, and Sora, was promoted to CTO in 2022, and briefly served as interim CEO after the board dismissed Altman in November 2023, gaining full insight into OpenAI's model development and productization path. Such a group of people who know OpenAI best left to build a technical system from scratch, and the outside world expected a route marked by OpenAI's imprint. However, the most obvious technical source turned out to point to Chinese models, which could easily be interpreted as a dramatic signal: the people who knew OpenAI best ultimately chose the technical blueprint of Chinese models.

sentences.en: Murati joined OpenAI in 2018, participated in DALL-E, Codex, ChatGPT, and Sora, was promoted to CTO in 2022, and briefly served as interim CEO after the board dismissed Altman in November 2023, gaining full insight into OpenAI's model development and productization path.
sentences.zh: Murati 于 2018 年加入 OpenAI，参与了 DALL-E、Codex、ChatGPT、Sora，2022 年晋升为 CTO，并在 2023 年 11 月董事会解雇 Altman 后短暂担任临时 CEO，全面洞察 OpenAI 的模型开发和产品化路径。

sentences.en: Such a group of people who know OpenAI best left to build a technical system from scratch, and the outside world expected a route marked by OpenAI's imprint.
sentences.zh: 这样一群了解OpenAI的人最好就留下来从头开始构建一个技术体系，外界期待一条打上OpenAI印记的路线。

sentences.en: However, the most obvious technical source turned out to point to Chinese models, which could easily be interpreted as a dramatic signal: the people who knew OpenAI best ultimately chose the technical blueprint of Chinese models.
sentences.zh: 然而，最明显的技术来源竟然指向了中国模式，这很容易被解读为一个戏剧性的信号：最了解OpenAI的人最终选择了中国模式的技术蓝图。

blocks.html_en: But this conclusion needs to be analyzed carefully. OpenAI's recent cutting-edge models are all closed-source, so the outside world cannot obtain weights or understand the complete architecture and training plan. Even if Murati was familiar with internal technologies, she couldn't transfer OpenAI's commercial secrets into the new company; however, DeepSeek and Kimi have publicly released weights or technical reports, and their architectures, training methods, and toolchains can be legally researched and reused.

sentences.en: But this conclusion needs to be analyzed carefully.
sentences.zh: 但这个结论需要仔细分析。

sentences.en: OpenAI's recent cutting-edge models are all closed-source, so the outside world cannot obtain weights or understand the complete architecture and training plan.
sentences.zh: OpenAI最近的前沿模型都是闭源的，因此外界无法获取权重或了解完整的架构和训练计划。

sentences.en: Even if Murati was familiar with internal technologies, she couldn't transfer OpenAI's commercial secrets into the new company; however, DeepSeek and Kimi have publicly released weights or technical reports, and their architectures, training methods, and toolchains can be legally researched and reused.
sentences.zh: 即使Murati熟悉内部技术，她也不可能将OpenAI的商业机密转移到新公司；不过，DeepSeek和Kimi已经公开发布了权重或技术报告，其架构、训练方法和工具链都可以合法研究和复用。

blocks.html_en: Inkling itself is also an open-weight model, and for companies wanting to build an open ecosystem, adopting mature solutions from open models like DeepSeek and Kimi is a logical engineering choice. Today, developing an open model while completely avoiding the publicly available achievements of Chinese teams would require a higher cost of trial and error. Therefore, drawing inspiration from Chinese models cannot prove that Murati believes DeepSeek is superior to OpenAI. The differences in openness and the conditions for reference are completely different between the two, but it sends a clear signal: at least in the field of open weights, Chinese models have become an important reference for American AI teams.

sentences.en: Inkling itself is also an open-weight model, and for companies wanting to build an open ecosystem, adopting mature solutions from open models like DeepSeek and Kimi is a logical engineering choice.
sentences.zh: Inkling本身也是一个开放权重模型，对于想要构建开放生态系统的公司来说，采用DeepSeek和Kimi等开放模型的成熟解决方案是一个合乎逻辑的工程选择。

sentences.en: Today, developing an open model while completely avoiding the publicly available achievements of Chinese teams would require a higher cost of trial and error.
sentences.zh: 如今，开发一个开放模型，同时完全避免中国团队公开的成果，需要更高的试错成本。

sentences.en: Therefore, drawing inspiration from Chinese models cannot prove that Murati believes DeepSeek is superior to OpenAI.
sentences.zh: 因此，从中国模型中汲取灵感并不能证明Murati认为DeepSeek优于OpenAI。

sentences.en: The differences in openness and the conditions for reference are completely different between the two, but it sends a clear signal: at least in the field of open weights, Chinese models have become an important reference for American AI teams.
sentences.zh: 两者开放性的差异以及借鉴的条件完全不同，但却传递出一个明确的信号：至少在开放权重领域，中国模型已经成为美国AI团队的重要参考。

blocks.html_en: The real question is why Thinking Machines, having absorbed existing architectures and training experiences, produced an Inkling with weaker performance and higher prices. Murati has a top team, sufficient funds, and the latest NVIDIA training system. There is no reason for her to overlook these gaps, so the high-cost alternative might be a calculated choice.

sentences.en: The real question is why Thinking Machines, having absorbed existing architectures and training experiences, produced an Inkling with weaker performance and higher prices.
sentences.zh: 真正的问题是，为什么Thinking Machines在吸收了现有架构和训练经验的基础上，生产出了性能更弱、价格更高的Inkling。

sentences.en: Murati has a top team, sufficient funds, and the latest NVIDIA training system.
sentences.zh: Murati拥有顶级的团队、充足的资金以及最新的NVIDIA培训系统。

sentences.en: There is no reason for her to overlook these gaps, so the high-cost alternative might be a calculated choice.
sentences.zh: 她没有理由忽视这些差距，因此高成本的替代方案可能是经过深思熟虑的选择。

blocks.html_en: Considering the treatment Thinking Machines received, Inkling shouldn't just be a decent model. In July 2025, the company completed a $2 billion seed round before launching any product, with a valuation of $12 billion, and investors include a16z, NVIDIA, AMD, and Sequoia. Four months later, the next round of negotiations aimed for a valuation of up to $500 billion, although not officially announced, the company was already seen as a potential competitor to OpenAI and Anthropic. When it launched a product that performed worse than Chinese models and was even more expensive, it inevitably fell short of expectations. But from a business perspective, Murati may have never intended to win the benchmarks from the beginning.

sentences.en: Considering the treatment Thinking Machines received, Inkling shouldn't just be a decent model.
sentences.zh: 考虑到 Thinking Machines 受到的待遇，Inkling 不应该只是一个不错的模型。

sentences.en: In July 2025, the company completed a $2 billion seed round before launching any product, with a valuation of $12 billion, and investors include a16z, NVIDIA, AMD, and Sequoia.
sentences.zh: 2025年7月，该公司在推出任何产品之前完成了20亿美元的种子轮融资，估值为120亿美元，投资者包括a16z、NVIDIA、AMD和红杉资本。

sentences.en: Four months later, the next round of negotiations aimed for a valuation of up to $500 billion, although not officially announced, the company was already seen as a potential competitor to OpenAI and Anthropic.
sentences.zh: 四个月后，下一轮谈判目标估值高达 5000 亿美元，虽然尚未正式宣布，但该公司已被视为 OpenAI 和 Anthropic 的潜在竞争对手。

sentences.en: When it launched a product that performed worse than Chinese models and was even more expensive, it inevitably fell short of expectations.
sentences.zh: 当它推出一款性能比中国车型差、甚至更贵的产品时，不可避免地达不到预期。

sentences.en: But from a business perspective, Murati may have never intended to win the benchmarks from the beginning.
sentences.zh: 但从商业角度来看，穆拉蒂可能从一开始就没有打算赢得标杆。

blocks.html_en: The company emphasizes open weights, multimodality, and customizability, hoping that enterprises will use Tinker to fine-tune Inkling and turn it into specialized models such as customer service, coding assistants, and industry agents. Murati's bet is on a market that does not prioritize benchmark rankings. Enterprises care more about whether the model can be privately deployed, whether they can control the data, and whether they can continue training according to their own business. A large portion of this demand has been met by Chinese open models: OpenAI and Anthropic have long been closed-source, and after Meta's Llama4 did not meet expectations, it gradually reduced its open-source approach. Meanwhile, DeepSeek, Kimi, Qwen, and GLM continuously released weights, with performance increasingly close to American closed-source models and much lower prices.

sentences.en: The company emphasizes open weights, multimodality, and customizability, hoping that enterprises will use Tinker to fine-tune Inkling and turn it into specialized models such as customer service, coding assistants, and industry agents.
sentences.zh: 该公司强调开放权重、多模态和可定制性，希望企业使用Tinker对Inkling进行微调，将其变成客户服务、编码助手、行业代理等专业模型。

sentences.en: Murati's bet is on a market that does not prioritize benchmark rankings.
sentences.zh: 穆拉蒂的赌注是一个不优先考虑基准排名的市场。

sentences.en: Enterprises care more about whether the model can be privately deployed, whether they can control the data, and whether they can continue training according to their own business.
sentences.zh: 企业更关心模型是否可以私有部署，是否可以控制数据，是否可以根据自己的业务继续训练。

sentences.en: A large portion of this demand has been met by Chinese open models: OpenAI and Anthropic have long been closed-source, and after Meta's Llama4 did not meet expectations, it gradually reduced its open-source approach.
sentences.zh: 这一需求很大一部分已经被中国的开放模式满足了：OpenAI和Anthropic早已闭源，而Meta的Llama4没有达到预期后，也逐渐减少了开源的方式。

sentences.en: Meanwhile, DeepSeek, Kimi, Qwen, and GLM continuously released weights, with performance increasingly close to American closed-source models and much lower prices.
sentences.zh: 与此同时，DeepSeek、Kimi、Qwen、GLM不断发布权重，性能越来越接近美国闭源模型，价格也大幅下降。

blocks.html_en: US enterprises have quickly voted with their feet— according to data provided by OpenRouter to CNBC, since February 2026, the weekly share of Chinese models in tokens called by US enterprises through the platform has exceeded 30% every week, reaching as high as 46%, while the average share in the first half of 2025 was only 4.5%. Cursor tested multiple base models when developing Composer2 and finally chose Kimi K2.5, which had the strongest evaluation. Bridgewater Fund also fine-tuned Alibaba's Qwen through Tinker, achieving better results than some top closed-source models. Thus, Chinese open models have become a cost-saving shortcut for US enterprises, but this path is now under pressure.

sentences.en: US enterprises have quickly voted with their feet— according to data provided by OpenRouter to CNBC, since February 2026, the weekly share of Chinese models in tokens called by US enterprises through the platform has exceeded 30% every week, reaching as high as 46%, while the average share in the first half of 2025 was only 4.5%.
sentences.zh: 美国企业迅速用脚投票——根据OpenRouter向CNBC提供的数据，自2026年2月以来，美国企业通过该平台调用的代币中中国型号的周份额每周都超过30%，高达46%，而2025年上半年的平均份额仅为4.5%。

sentences.en: Cursor tested multiple base models when developing Composer2 and finally chose Kimi K2.5, which had the strongest evaluation.
sentences.zh: Cursor在开发Composer2时测试了多个基础模型，最终选择了评价最强的Kimi K2.5。

sentences.en: Bridgewater Fund also fine-tuned Alibaba's Qwen through Tinker, achieving better results than some top closed-source models.
sentences.zh: 桥水基金还通过Tinker对阿里巴巴的Qwen进行了微调，取得了比一些顶级闭源模型更好的结果。

sentences.en: Thus, Chinese open models have become a cost-saving shortcut for US enterprises, but this path is now under pressure.
sentences.zh: 由此可见，中国开放模式已成为美国企业节省成本的捷径，但这条道路目前面临着压力。

blocks.html_en: At the same time, the regulatory atmosphere in the US toward Chinese AI models is tightening. Relevant departments have issued warnings about data security, model sources, and supply chain risks, and some companies using Chinese models have faced investigations. Even without unified restrictions, policy uncertainty has been enough to affect enterprise choices, especially for companies involving government business and sensitive data.

sentences.en: At the same time, the regulatory atmosphere in the US toward Chinese AI models is tightening.
sentences.zh: 与此同时，美国对中国人工智能模型的监管氛围正在收紧。

sentences.en: Relevant departments have issued warnings about data security, model sources, and supply chain risks, and some companies using Chinese models have faced investigations.
sentences.zh: 有关部门就数据安全、模型来源、供应链风险等方面发出警告，一些使用中国模型的企业面临调查。

sentences.en: Even without unified restrictions, policy uncertainty has been enough to affect enterprise choices, especially for companies involving government business and sensitive data.
sentences.zh: 即使没有统一限制，政策的不确定性也足以影响企业的选择，尤其是涉及政府业务和敏感数据的企业。

blocks.html_en: This is exactly the market space for Inkling: it is an open-weight model developed by an American company, using the Apache 2.0 license, supporting private deployment and customization. US enterprises choose it, without worrying about political controversies caused by using Chinese models, and it is easier to pass compliance reviews from government clients and large companies. The real value of Inkling comes from this compliance certainty— Murati transformed the public achievements of DeepSeek and Kimi into a model provided by an American company. It doesn't need to defeat Chinese models in benchmarks, but only needs to be an acceptable choice for US enterprises that dare not continue using Chinese models. This also explains why it dares to set a higher price: the policy has narrowed the competitive scope, and the gap in performance and price is less fatal. For some US enterprises, a slightly weaker and more expensive model is already sufficient.

sentences.en: This is exactly the market space for Inkling: it is an open-weight model developed by an American company, using the Apache 2.0 license, supporting private deployment and customization.
sentences.zh: 这正是Inkling的市场空间：它是一家美国公司开发的开放权重模型，使用Apache 2.0许可证，支持私有部署和定制。

sentences.en: US enterprises choose it, without worrying about political controversies caused by using Chinese models, and it is easier to pass compliance reviews from government clients and large companies.
sentences.zh: 美国企业选择它，不用担心使用中国模式引发政治争议，而且更容易通过政府客户和大公司的合规审查。

sentences.en: The real value of Inkling comes from this compliance certainty— Murati transformed the public achievements of DeepSeek and Kimi into a model provided by an American company.
sentences.zh: Inkling 的真正价值来自于这种合规确定性——Murati 将 DeepSeek 和 Kimi 的公开成果转化为美国公司提供的模型。

sentences.en: It doesn't need to defeat Chinese models in benchmarks, but only needs to be an acceptable choice for US enterprises that dare not continue using Chinese models.
sentences.zh: 不需要在对标中击败中国模式，只需要成为不敢继续使用中国模式的美国企业可以接受的选择即可。

sentences.en: This also explains why it dares to set a higher price: the policy has narrowed the competitive scope, and the gap in performance and price is less fatal.
sentences.zh: 这也解释了为什么它敢于定出更高的价格：政策缩小了竞争范围，性能和价格的差距不那么致命。

sentences.en: For some US enterprises, a slightly weaker and more expensive model is already sufficient.
sentences.zh: 对于一些美国企业来说，稍微弱一些、更贵的模式就已经足够了。

content_html_zh:
OpenAI前首席技术官Murati离开前雇主后的第一份工作，意外地颠倒了中国和美国在大型模型开发中的角色。 7月15日，她创立了Thinking Machines Lab，并发布了第一个模型Inkling。 混合专家架构主要遵循DeepSeek-V3，对于训练后冷启动，它还使用了Moonshot的Kimi K2.5等开放模型生成的合成数据。 结果相当微妙：在多项基准评估中，Inkling 落后于 Kimi 和 GLM，而且其调用价格甚至更高。 一家融资20亿美元、估值120亿美元的明星公司，上市首秀有些低于预期，但穆拉蒂可能从一开始就没打算争夺头把交椅。

单从硬件配置来看，Inkling确实不愧是明星实验室的形象。 它采用混合专家架构，总参数数为 9750 亿个，每个处理的代币激活 410 亿个参数；预训练使用了45万亿个token，涵盖文本、图像、音频和视频，支持高达100万个token的上下文；它可以理解文本、图像和音频，以文本形式输出，并支持调整思维强度以平衡性能、速度和成本。 该模型还开放了权重，使用相对宽松的 Apache 2.0 许可证，允许开发者下载权重进行自我部署或通过 Thinking Machines 的 Tinker 平台进行微调。 该公司的定位很明确：与其直接与普通用户的ChatGPT竞争，Inkling更适合作为企业开发AI应用的基础。

真正引起关注的，隐藏在技术报告的后半部分。 Thinking Machines在架构介绍中明确表示，Inkling的混合专家设计主要沿用了DeepSeek-V3，拥有大量的专家模块，每个token只激活一小部分，并且还采用了DeepSeek-V3的无辅助丢失负载均衡设计。 中国模型的影响延伸到了训练阶段：为了启动后期训练，该公司首先使用开放权重模型生成的合成数据进行一轮监督微调，明确提到了 Kimi K2.5。

完整的路线图浮出水面——OpenAI前CTO创立的美国实验室，其第一个模型遵循DeepSeek架构，然后用Kimi的数据开始后期训练。 借用开放模型本身并没有问题，因为 DeepSeek 和 Kimi 公开发布权重并允许使用，这是建立在公共成果的基础上的。 更尴尬的是，在吸收了中国模特的技术后，Inkling仍然没有超越这些老师。 Thinking Machines 也坦言：Inkling 并不是目前最强的模型，无论是开源的还是闭源的。

当数字清晰地列出时，它就变得更加清晰。 在最终的真人考试纯文本评估中，Inkling 得分为 29.7%，而 Kimi K2.6 和 GLM 5.2 分别达到 35.9% 和 40.1%；添加工具后，Inkling上升至46%，仍低于Kimi K2.6的54%和GLM 5.2的54.7%；在测试真实软件工程能力的SWE-Bench Pro中，Inkling得分为54.3%，而Kimi K2.6为58.6%，GLM 5.2达到62.1%；差距在 Terminal Bench 2.1 中最为明显，Inkling 得分为 63.8%，而 Kimi K2.6 和 GLM 5.2 分别为 71.3% 和 82.7%。

当然，Inkling 在 Web 应用程序设计、音频理解和安全评估方面具有优势，在某些数学任务中甚至可以超过 Kimi 和 DeepSeek。 但在综合推理、编程、代理能力方面，还没有进入开放模型的顶尖水平。 其定价也没有带来惊喜：在 Tinker 平台上，64K 版本的收费为每 100 万个 Prefill Token 1.87 美元，每 100 万个 Sample Token 收费 4.68 美元，而且这已经是五天折扣价了。 256K 版本分别上涨至 3.74 美元和 9.36 美元；相比Kimi K2.6官方API输入0.95美元、输出4美元，以及GLM 5.2的1.4美元和4.4美元，Inkling的预充价格是Kimi K2.6的近两倍，其代价也更高，没有表现出明显的价格优势。 这样一来，情况就变得有些微妙了：架构参考了DeepSeek，后期训练依赖Kimi，整体性能没有超过国内领先的开放模型，而且调用价格更高。

之所以有趣，是因为思维机器的独特背景。 该公司几乎是一个 OpenAI 校友会——2025 年 2 月成立时，团队约有 30 人，三分之二来自 OpenAI，其余主要来自 Meta 和 Mistral。 创始团队曾包括 John Schulman、Barret Zoph、Lilian Weng、Andrew Tulloch 和 Luke Metz，据路透社报道，Murati 从她的前雇主那里招募了至少 20 名研究人员。

Murati 于 2018 年加入 OpenAI，参与了 DALL-E、Codex、ChatGPT、Sora，2022 年晋升为 CTO，并在 2023 年 11 月董事会解雇 Altman 后短暂担任临时 CEO，全面洞察 OpenAI 的模型开发和产品化路径。 这样一群了解OpenAI的人最好就留下来从头开始构建一个技术体系，外界期待一条打上OpenAI印记的路线。 然而，最明显的技术来源竟然指向了中国模式，这很容易被解读为一个戏剧性的信号：最了解OpenAI的人最终选择了中国模式的技术蓝图。

但这个结论需要仔细分析。 OpenAI最近的前沿模型都是闭源的，因此外界无法获取权重或了解完整的架构和训练计划。 即使Murati熟悉内部技术，她也不可能将OpenAI的商业机密转移到新公司；不过，DeepSeek和Kimi已经公开发布了权重或技术报告，其架构、训练方法和工具链都可以合法研究和复用。

Inkling本身也是一个开放权重模型，对于想要构建开放生态系统的公司来说，采用DeepSeek和Kimi等开放模型的成熟解决方案是一个合乎逻辑的工程选择。 如今，开发一个开放模型，同时完全避免中国团队公开的成果，需要更高的试错成本。 因此，从中国模型中汲取灵感并不能证明Murati认为DeepSeek优于OpenAI。 两者开放性的差异以及借鉴的条件完全不同，但却传递出一个明确的信号：至少在开放权重领域，中国模型已经成为美国AI团队的重要参考。

真正的问题是，为什么Thinking Machines在吸收了现有架构和训练经验的基础上，生产出了性能更弱、价格更高的Inkling。 Murati拥有顶级的团队、充足的资金以及最新的NVIDIA培训系统。 她没有理由忽视这些差距，因此高成本的替代方案可能是经过深思熟虑的选择。

考虑到 Thinking Machines 受到的待遇，Inkling 不应该只是一个不错的模型。 2025年7月，该公司在推出任何产品之前完成了20亿美元的种子轮融资，估值为120亿美元，投资者包括a16z、NVIDIA、AMD和红杉资本。 四个月后，下一轮谈判目标估值高达 5000 亿美元，虽然尚未正式宣布，但该公司已被视为 OpenAI 和 Anthropic 的潜在竞争对手。 当它推出一款性能比中国车型差、甚至更贵的产品时，不可避免地达不到预期。 但从商业角度来看，穆拉蒂可能从一开始就没有打算赢得标杆。

该公司强调开放权重、多模态和可定制性，希望企业使用Tinker对Inkling进行微调，将其变成客户服务、编码助手、行业代理等专业模型。 穆拉蒂的赌注是一个不优先考虑基准排名的市场。 企业更关心模型是否可以私有部署，是否可以控制数据，是否可以根据自己的业务继续训练。 这一需求很大一部分已经被中国的开放模式满足了：OpenAI和Anthropic早已闭源，而Meta的Llama4没有达到预期后，也逐渐减少了开源的方式。 与此同时，DeepSeek、Kimi、Qwen、GLM不断发布权重，性能越来越接近美国闭源模型，价格也大幅下降。

美国企业迅速用脚投票——根据OpenRouter向CNBC提供的数据，自2026年2月以来，美国企业通过该平台调用的代币中中国型号的周份额每周都超过30%，高达46%，而2025年上半年的平均份额仅为4.5%。 Cursor在开发Composer2时测试了多个基础模型，最终选择了评价最强的Kimi K2.5。 桥水基金还通过Tinker对阿里巴巴的Qwen进行了微调，取得了比一些顶级闭源模型更好的结果。 由此可见，中国开放模式已成为美国企业节省成本的捷径，但这条道路目前面临着压力。

与此同时，美国对中国人工智能模型的监管氛围正在收紧。 有关部门就数据安全、模型来源、供应链风险等方面发出警告，一些使用中国模型的企业面临调查。 即使没有统一限制，政策的不确定性也足以影响企业的选择，尤其是涉及政府业务和敏感数据的企业。

这正是Inkling的市场空间：它是一家美国公司开发的开放权重模型，使用Apache 2.0许可证，支持私有部署和定制。 美国企业选择它，不用担心使用中国模式引发政治争议，而且更容易通过政府客户和大公司的合规审查。 Inkling 的真正价值来自于这种合规确定性——Murati 将 DeepSeek 和 Kimi 的公开成果转化为美国公司提供的模型。 不需要在对标中击败中国模式，只需要成为不敢继续使用中国模式的美国企业可以接受的选择即可。 这也解释了为什么它敢于定出更高的价格：政策缩小了竞争范围，性能和价格的差距不那么致命。 对于一些美国企业来说，稍微弱一些、更贵的模式就已经足够了。
